title: where to buy NGE series
wikitext:
[]: where to buy NGE series             Quote Post by dcelf » Sat Jul 27, 2013 10:04 am when i first watched the original NGE series it was at a friends house and now i would like to buy them, and he wont sell it lol. i got 1.11 and 2.22 from the walmart website, but they dont have the series, so i looked around online some more(because nobody sells it locally) for the the 26 episode neon genesis evangelion DVD set and i cant find anywhere to buy them. can someone reccomend a site or a link to a website where i can buy it and have it shipped to me? im sorry if this is a dumb question, but i just really need to know. thanks

[]:             Quote Post by Veefy » Sat Jul 27, 2013 11:07 am Tv series is out of print since November 2011? I believe. Might have to be patient on eBay if you don't want to spend top dollar to get them. amazon prices reflect out of print status. I'd say ebay is your best bet.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Jul 27, 2013 11:46 am eBay has a lot of cool stuff on it.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Sat Jul 27, 2013 5:09 pm Amazon also still has it in stock, though at this point you might find better prices on eBay.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sun Jul 28, 2013 1:24 am Righstuf has the Platinum Complete for $75 and at that price point you've got free shipping for a US order.

[]:             Quote Post by dcelf » Sun Jul 28, 2013 3:34 am just bought it, $75 on rightstuff . wow, thats the most ive ever spent on something that you watch on TV... anyways thanks so much for recommending the website, you were really helpful

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sun Jul 28, 2013 8:43 pm You're welcome, and welcome to the forum.

[]:             Quote Post by Jawmuncher » Wed Jul 31, 2013 7:44 pm AR-99 wrote: Righstuf has the Platinum Complete for $75 and at that price point you've got free shipping for a US order.

[]:             Quote Post by Veefy » Thu Aug 01, 2013 2:48 am Jawmuncher wrote: Still kinda wish someone would pick it up for distribution again on DVD

[]:             Quote Post by Melchior » Thu Aug 01, 2013 2:47 pm I'd like to think (admittedly, as the cash cow it is... ) that there will be some gnarly re-release of the series and of EoE... MAYBE, even a remastered *higher* definition version of it. Wishful thinking on my part... but, I have a lot of wishful thoughts regarding Evangelion in general. Those, I will NOT be going on a tangent for.

[]:             Quote Post by Nuclear Lunchbox » Thu Aug 01, 2013 8:02 pm Try King Roach Enterprises. I know that they have it, but I don't remember how expensive it is.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Thu Aug 01, 2013 11:28 pm They have it for $72 and add $6.55 shipping.

[]:             Quote Post by Jawmuncher » Tue Oct 01, 2013 6:48 am Melchior wrote: I'd like to think (admittedly, as the cash cow it is... ) that there will be some gnarly re-release of the series and of EoE... MAYBE, even a remastered *higher* definition version of it. Wishful thinking on my part... but, I have a lot of wishful thoughts regarding Evangelion in general. Those, I will NOT be going on a tangent for.

[]:             Quote Post by Melchior » Tue Oct 01, 2013 10:39 am Jawmuncher wrote: The chance of that happening is the only thing keeping me from jumping on a platinum box set ATM. But with not even a slightest of murmurs or rumors I think I might just grab a Platinum collection again. (Sorry for the very late reply)

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Tue Oct 01, 2013 11:30 am An update, Rightstuf has jacked the Platinum back to full price at $90 and excluded it from coupon discounts. I would get it myself at one point, but already having the Perfect Collection and DC DVDs doesn't provide much motivation and I prioritize getting other Eva merch first. Not trying to collect every video iteration of the series, nearly did that with Battle Royale and that was enough.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Tue Oct 01, 2013 2:39 pm I got lucky and got Platinum for about 30 bucks or so on Amazon while it was still in-print, while I found a spare copy of Perfect Collection at a Sam's Goodly near my home area for, if I recall, 70 to 90 bucks (I bought Perfect first, thinking that was my only chance of getting the show on DVD). To this day (other than a lone Utena DVD), it's the only anime series I own with two different copies. I'll probably keep Perfect if I wanted a reminder for what Eva looked like when it first aired.

[]:             Quote Post by pwhodges » Tue Oct 01, 2013 3:14 pm Melchior wrote: The Platinum set is still worth it - it's a collectible at this rate. When it's extinct, it will definitely be worth having in the collection.

[]:             Quote Post by ath » Tue Oct 01, 2013 5:27 pm C.A.P. wrote: I got lucky and got Platinum for about 30 bucks or so on Amazon while it was still in-print

[]:             Quote Post by ZapX » Tue Oct 01, 2013 5:40 pm I have the Platinum Complete thinpak and the Death/Rebirth & EoE box set I may be willing to part with if anyone wants them... Anyone care to make an offer for one or both?

[]:             Quote Post by Kazuki_Fuse » Wed Oct 02, 2013 6:52 pm I bought the Platinum set in "used condition" (the girl selling it had watched through it once) for $50 at a convention when I was 14. I bought it because I loved the series and had money to burn, but I distinctly remember regretting the purchase for a few hours afterwards as I had no money left for anything else. But looking back, HOLY SHIT am I ever glad I picked it up when I did. I had no idea until about 2 years ago that it was actually a chore to try and own the original series, I just assumed because of its huge influence and relative popularity that any place that sold anime would have it. To anyone who thinks spending $75-$100 is expensive, just remember this quote taken from the fourth omnibus manga of NGE: "Back in 1998, $30 for a two-episode VHS was pretty standard for the U.S. anime industry, not just the Evangelion series. So that meant you'd have to pay $390 for the whole series (in late-90's dollars, with inflation that'd be around $500!) And that was for VHS format, with all its built in limitations compared to DVD- no special features, no chapter skipping, no seperate audio or subtitle tracks, and of course, lesser video and audio quality. Its something to think about next time a DVD set seems expensive." - Carl Gustav Horn
